title: Snap Q2 2022 Earnings: Revenue Miss
text:
Snap Q2 2022 Earnings: Revenue Miss

Snap reported disappointing second-quarter results, with $1.11 billion in revenue, falling short of previous guidance. The figure represented just 13% year-over-year growth, well below the company's projected 20-25% increase.

Daily active users grew 18% annually to 347 million, but the company declined to provide third-quarter guidance, citing uncertainties related to the operating environment. Snap shares dropped 23% in after-hours trading following the announcement.

Leadership Response

CEO Evan Spiegel acknowledged the shortfall, stating the company is evolving our business and strategy to reaccelerate revenue growth, including innovating on our products, investing heavily in our direct response advertising business.

Cost-Cutting Measures

The company plans a substantially reduced rate of hiring while examining operating expenses. Spiegel and co-founder Bobby Murphy extended their contracts through January 2027, each accepting $1 annual compensation with no equity benefits. The company also authorized a $500 million stock buyback program.

Market Pressures

Snap attributed the slowdown to macroeconomic headwinds, including inflation, supply chain issues, and increased advertising competition. CFO Derek Andersen noted advertisers can easily scale campaigns up or down on the platform, amplifying revenue volatility.

Strategic Pivots

The company launched Snapchat+, a $3.99 monthly subscription service, and introduced a web version offering messaging and video calls, diversifying beyond its advertising-dependent model.
